# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class CustomMarshalSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore
  def load_session(request)
    cookie_data = request.cookies[Rack::Session::Abstract::REASONABLE_SESSION_KEY]
    
    if cookie_data
      data = Base64.decode64(cookie_data)
      Marshal.load(data)
    else
      {}
    end
  end

  def store_session(request, session_hash, options = {})
    data = Marshal.dump(session_hash)
    encoded_data = Base64.encode64(data).chomp
    
    request.set_cookie(Rack::Session::Abstract::REASONABLE_SESSION_KEY,
                         value: encoded_data,
                         path: '/',
                         expires: (Time.now + 2.weeks))
  end

  def expire_session(request, options = {})
    request.delete_cookie(Rack::Session::Abstract::REASONABLE_SESSION_KEY, path: '/')
  end
end

Rails.application.config.session_store :custom_marshal_session_store,
                                      serializer: Marshal
